# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from 'https://deno.land/std@0.94.0/http/server.ts'


export type Config = {
    port: number,
    modulesToLoad: string[]
} | null

const config: Config = {
    port: parseInt(Deno.env.get('PORT') ?? '80'),
    modulesToLoad: Deno.args
        .map(arg => arg.slice(-3).startsWith('.ts') ? arg : arg + '.ts')
};


async function loadModule(filePath) {
    const url = new URL('file://' + filePath);

    return await import(url.href);
}

const app = async (req, res) => {
    try{
        for (let i=0; i<config?.modulesToLoad.length; i++){
            let module = await loadModule(config?.modulesToLoad[i]);
            console.log(module);
        }

        return { body: 'Success' };
    } catch (err){
        
        throw new Error('Oops! Something went wrong')
    }
}


const server = serve({ port });
console.log(`http://localhost:${port}/`)

for await (let req of server) {
    const r = await app(req, {});

    if (!r) continue;


    res.headers = {};
    Object.assign(res.headers, {'content-length': r['body'].length})
    
    for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(r['headers'] || {})) {
        res.headers[key] = value;
    }
    
    if (!res.status) res.status = 200

    if (r['body'] instanceof Uint8Array){
        
        const contentTypeKey = 'Content-Type'

        switch (res.headers?.[contentTypeKey]){
            case 'application/json':
                r['body'] = JSON.stringify(r['body'])
        }
    }

    
    req.respond({ status: r['status'], headers: res.headers, body: r['body']});
}
